channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Official New York Knicks 2026 NBA Championship Merchandise Released Following Finals Victory
item.description: Following the New York Knicks' 94-90 victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the 2026 NBA Finals, the franchise secured its first NBA championship since 1973 and only the third title in team h...

item.title: Mike Sonko Showcases Luxury Vehicle Fleet at Daughter Janetta Nyamu's Traditional Wedding in Machakos
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko and his wife, Primrose Mbuvi, hosted the traditional wedding of their daughter Janetta Nyamu on June 13, 2026, at their home in Mua Hills, Machakos County. Janetta m...

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes in Beirut Raise Concerns About Prospects for a U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement
item.description: A report citing Al Jazeera suggests that ongoing military tensions in the Middle East could jeopardize efforts by the United States and Iran to reach a diplomatic agreement. According to the article, ...

item.title: Amazon Stock Pullback Spurs Debate Over Valuation and Long-Term Growth Potential
item.description: The article analyzes Amazon.com’s recent stock performance and evaluates whether current valuation models suggest the stock is undervalued. Amazon shares closed at approximately $238.55, reflecting a ...

item.title: Opinion Writer Marks Trump's 80th Birthday With a Satirical List of 80 Mock Gifts
item.description: This opinion article by journalist Ray Richmond commemorates President Donald Trump's 80th birthday through satire and political criticism. Rather than offering praise, the author presents a list of 8...

item.title: Joyce Vance Says Future Prosecutors Should Evaluate Potential Crimes by Trump Allies Based on Evidence and Standard Legal Procedures
item.description: Former federal prosecutor Joyce Vance discussed the possibility that officials and allies who helped carry out President Donald Trump's agenda could face criminal investigations after his administrati...

item.title: Airtel Money Handles US$196 Billion in Transactions as Financial Inclusion Grows Across Africa
item.description: Airtel Africa has reported strong growth in its mobile financial services business, with Airtel Money processing transactions worth US$196 billion during the 2025/26 financial year. According to the c...

item.title: Ramaphosa Announces Immigration Measures Amid Rising Xenophobic Tensions and Criticism of Existing Policies
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has unveiled a five-part strategy aimed at addressing illegal immigration while condemning xenophobic groups that have fueled anti-foreigner protests across the...

item.title: Boy George’s Long Creative Partnership with Virgin Records Through the 1980s and 1990s
item.description: The article explores the long and influential relationship between British pop icon Boy George and Virgin Records, spanning more than four decades and reaching its peak during the 1980s and 1990s. It ...

item.title: Gerda Steyn claims fifth Comrades Marathon victory with new up-run record
item.description: Gerda Steyn delivered another dominant performance at the Comrades Marathon on Sunday, winning the women’s race from Durban to Pietermaritzburg and securing her fifth overall title in the iconic ultra...

item.title: Photographs by Adrian Gee Document Everyday Life in Cornwall During the 1960s
item.description: This article presents a collection of vintage photographs taken by photographer Adrian Gee that document daily life in Cornwall, England, during the 1960s. Rather than focusing on the region's famous ...

item.title: Chicago Health Officials Detect First West Nile Virus-Positive Mosquitoes of 2026, No Human Cases Reported
item.description: The Chicago Department of Public Health announced that mosquitoes carrying West Nile virus have been identified in Chicago for the first time in 2026. While infected mosquitoes have now been detected ...

item.title: Norilsk: From Soviet Gulag to One of the World's Most Polluted Arctic Industrial Cities
item.description: The article explores the history, environment, and unique social reality of Norilsk, a remote industrial city in northern Siberia located more than 300 kilometers above the Arctic Circle. Norilsk was ...

item.title: Democratic Attorneys General Say They Have Prepared Legal Responses to Potential Federal Election Interference
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta said that a coalition of Democratic attorneys general has been preparing contingency plans for a range of election-related scenarios that they believe could arise...

item.title: Thomas T. Hammond’s Photographic Record of Everyday Life Across the Soviet Union During the Cold War
item.description: The article examines the historical significance of photographs taken by American historian and University of Virginia professor Thomas T. Hammond during multiple trips to the Soviet Union between the...

item.title: Ghana formally protests Canada's visa denial for Thomas Partey ahead of World Cup match
item.description: Ghana has formally protested Canada's decision to deny a visa to national team player Thomas Partey, preventing him from travelling to Toronto for Ghana's opening 2026 FIFA World Cup match against Pan...

item.title: Thousands of Australian and Turkish supporters create festive atmosphere at Vancouver’s first 2026 World Cup match
item.description: Vancouver hosted its first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Saturday, drawing large and enthusiastic crowds of international supporters, particularly from Australia and Turkey. Estimates of Austral...

item.title: Chinese Study Highlights Redundancy Advantages of Multi-Engine Lunar Lander Designs Over Single-Engine Architectures
item.description: A study published in the journal Chinese Space Science and Technology argues that NASA’s lunar landing architecture relies heavily on a single-engine propulsion system, creating a potential single poi...

item.title: Conservative Host Claims Sabotage After Algae Returns to Renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: A conservative media host, Grant Stinchfield, has suggested that left-wing saboteurs are responsible for the green algae that appeared in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., shor...

item.title: Harper Beckham's Attempt to Visit Brooklyn Beckham in Los Angeles Ends Without a Meeting During Ongoing Family Rift
item.description: Harper Beckham, the 14-year-old daughter of David and Victoria Beckham, reportedly attempted to visit her older brother Brooklyn Beckham at his Los Angeles home following celebrations honoring David B...
